overview.description: Baneswar Shiva temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located at Baneswar in the Cooch Behar II CD block in the Cooch Behar Sadar subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in West Bengal, India. Here, Shiva is worshipped in the form of Baneswar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Baneshwar Shiva temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Baneshwar Shiva temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Baneshwar Shiva temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bagdogra Airport (Siliguri/Darjeeling) (120 km) and the nearest railway station is Alipurduar Junction (10 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Baneshwar Shiva temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Baneshwar Shiva temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Baneshwar Shiva temple?
faq.a: Stays in Baneshwar Shiva temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Baneshwar Shiva temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Baneswar, Sidheswari, Baikunthapur, Cooch Behar II, Khagrabari.
